creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Charles P from comment #0)
> This was added quite some time ago, in 2014, for no reason that I can
> tell just from the commit.
The reason is what commit log for ports r372636 says (and which you understood correctly): to save games' passwords so they can be reloaded again upon restart.  Alex Kushnaryov was running 24-hour OpenTTD server and got tired of players losing their passwords whenever the server required restart.  He saw the patch posted on some OpenTTD forum, made a few improvements, and sent it to me; he didn't try to upstream it because he was not the original author.

> Even though this patch is guarded by a WITH_SAVE_PASSWORDS define,
> it feels very wrong that the official port should make any changes to
> the functionality of the program, and certainly not one that saves
> passwords out in plain text to an arbitrary file.
First and foremost, just as you say, this feature is disabled by default, in accordance with the upstream policy to not store clear-text user passwords anywhere.  However, there's nothing in the OpenTTD license that prevents making modifications to the original bits we offer as FreeBSD port; in fact, this is how open source generally works and improves itself.

I see your point, but at the end of the day it's a trade-off between security and usability, and for many people it's worth the risk, hence they keep asking for it*.

> (Incidentally, OTTD will likely have some actual password saving
> feature for the next major release with actual cryptographically
> secure storage, but that work is still ongoing)
Good; so when this happens, we may drop the "insecure" optional patch. :-)

*) https://github.com/OpenTTD/OpenTTD/issues/9713
